hypotheses.name: Outage Directly Impacting Customer
hypotheses.statement: Incident '{incident_id}' (Severity {severity}) by '{owning_tenant_name}' is directly causing SLI degradation for the customer — the incident's impact timeline aligns with observed SLI breaches.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-OUT-002 (confirmed outage, IsOutage=true) and SYM-OUT-003 (time overlap within 60 min) co-occur with SLI breach symptoms. SYM-SUP-005 (multiple customers filing tickets) strongly reinforces platform-wide impact. High severity and active status increase confidence.

hypotheses.name: Outage Compounding Pre-Existing Issue
hypotheses.statement: Incident '{incident_id}' by '{owning_tenant_name}' is compounding a pre-existing issue — the customer had SLI degradation before the incident's ImpactStartDate ({impact_start_date}).
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-SLI-001/SYM-SLI-002 show EarliestImpactStart before the incident's ImpactStartDate, and SYM-SLI-003 (multi-SLI breach) suggests broader degradation predating the outage.

hypotheses.name: Widespread Outage with Child Incidents
hypotheses.statement: Incident '{incident_id}' (Severity {severity}) by '{owning_tenant_name}' has {child_count} child incidents, indicating a widespread platform issue affecting multiple teams or services.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-OUT-002 (confirmed outage) co-occurs with high ChildCount and SYM-SLI-004 (cross-subscription) or SYM-SLI-005 (cross-region) patterns, confirming broad blast radius.

hypotheses.name: Incident Correlated with Customer Support Case
hypotheses.statement: Incident '{incident_id}' by '{owning_tenant_name}' is linked to support ticket '{support_ticket_id}', directly correlating a platform incident with a customer complaint — the incident is the likely root cause of the reported issue.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-OUT-004 (customer-correlated incident with SupportTicketId) co-occurs with SYM-SUP-001/SYM-SUP-002 (support case filed or CritSit), confirming the customer actively reported the issue.

hypotheses.name: Incident Not Affecting Customer (False Positive)
hypotheses.statement: Incident '{incident_id}' (Severity {severity}, Status: {status}) by '{owning_tenant_name}' is NOT impacting the customer — SLI data shows no degradation correlated with the incident timeline.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest when SYM-OUT-001 exists (incident detected) but SYM-OUT-002 (confirmed outage) is absent, SYM-OUT-003 (time overlap) is absent, and no SLI breach symptoms fire for the customer's subscriptions.
